skill.name: ai-ethics
skill.description: Evaluate AI bias, fairness, transparency, privacy, governance, human oversight, and regulatory readiness for responsible AI systems.
skill.summary: Assess and document responsible AI risks across fairness, transparency, governance, privacy, and human oversight.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks and weak-crypto keywords, but review found false positives only. The skill is a documentation-only AI ethics guide with no executable code, network behavior, prompt injection, or secret access evidence.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Lines 201-202 use Markdown backticks around reference file names, not Ruby or shell backtick execution. No command, interpolation, or executable instruction is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is a Markdown reference list containing file names. There is no executable code or command syntax at the cited lines.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The cited lines contain AI ethics prose and headings, not cryptographic algorithms or implementation guidance. No weak hash, cipher, or encryption code appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are ordinary documentation text such as descriptions, headings, and checklist items. Review found no cryptographic operation or recommendation in that context.

content.value_statement: AI teams need clear checks for bias, fairness, transparency, privacy, and governance risk. This skill provides structured review guidance for responsible AI assessments and documentation.
actual_capabilities: Lists common AI bias types and mitigation strategies.
Explains fairness metrics for group and individual fairness reviews.
Outlines transparency methods including SHAP, LIME, attention maps, and model cards.
Summarizes governance practices for risk assessment, documentation, and review workflows.
Covers human oversight patterns, privacy-preserving techniques, and environmental impact considerations.
limitations: Does not run automated bias tests or compute fairness metrics.
Does not include executable scripts, datasets, or compliance templates.
Does not replace legal advice for AI regulation.
Does not verify a model or dataset without user-provided evidence.

use_cases.title: Review Model Fairness
use_cases.description: Use the skill to identify bias sources, select fairness metrics, and plan mitigation before deployment.

use_cases.title: Prepare Governance Documentation
use_cases.description: Use the skill to structure model cards, risk records, approval workflows, and incident response documentation.

use_cases.title: Assess Human Oversight
use_cases.description: Use the skill to decide when humans should approve, monitor, or override AI system outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Identify Ethics Risks
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review for a planned AI feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this AI feature for ethical risks. Focus on fairness, privacy, transparency, safety, accountability, and human control.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Bias Assessment
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review for a model with known user groups.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a bias assessment plan for this model. Include protected groups, likely bias sources, fairness metrics, and mitigation options.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Model Card Sections
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft model card sections for this AI system. Include intended use, training data, subgroup performance, limitations, ethical risks, and update history.

prompt_templates.title: Map Governance Controls
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced governance planning for high-impact AI.
prompt_templates.prompt: Map governance controls for this AI system. Include risk level, approval steps, monitoring, human oversight, audit trails, and incident response.

output_examples.input: Assess a resume screening model for ethical concerns.
output_examples.output: The review should examine historical hiring bias, representation gaps, proxy variables, subgroup performance, explainability, appeal paths, and human review for rejected candidates.

output_examples.input: Create a transparency checklist for a healthcare triage assistant.
output: Define intended clinical use and excluded uses.
Document training data sources and known gaps.
Report performance by relevant patient subgroup.
Explain when clinicians must review or override recommendations.

output_examples.input: Plan privacy controls for an AI customer support tool.
output_examples.output: Recommended controls include data minimization, retention limits, anonymization where possible, access controls, audit logs, and clear user disclosure.

best_practices: Provide system purpose, affected groups, data sources, and deployment context before requesting an assessment.
Use multiple fairness metrics because one metric can hide harmful subgroup effects.
Document human oversight, escalation paths, and limits before production release.
anti_patterns: Do not treat a high overall accuracy score as proof that the system is fair.
Do not rely on explainability tools without validating whether users can understand the explanation.
Do not deploy high-impact AI without clear ownership, monitoring, and incident response.

faq.question: Can this skill run fairness tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides assessment guidance, but it does not execute tests or calculate metrics.

faq.question: Does this skill provide legal advice?
faq.answer: No. It can help organize regulatory questions, but legal counsel should review binding obligations.

faq.question: Which AI systems benefit most from this skill?
faq.answer: It is most useful for systems that affect people, rights, access, safety, employment, credit, healthcare, or public services.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What inputs should users provide?
faq.answer: Provide model purpose, users, affected groups, data sources, decision impact, deployment setting, and known constraints.

faq.question: Does the skill include executable code?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill is a Markdown guide and does not include executable scripts.
